subject: A wickedly curved scythe with a wrapped haft, suspended or planted at the center of a stone chamber lit by candles in iron sconces
description: A long, ominous scythe dominates the frame, its blade a dark serrated crescent and its haft bound in cloth wrappings. The weapon is mounted or floating in the center of a recessed alcove of grey stone bricks. Four orange-red candles in iron wall sconces flicker around it, throwing warm flame against the otherwise cold, desaturated masonry. The blade catches a faint highlight; the scene reads as a ritual reliquary or shrine where the weapon is venerated rather than wielded. No figures are present — the scythe itself is the subject, framed like an altar piece.
foreground: curved scythe with wrapped haft, blade prominent
background: stone alcove with brick masonry and four candle sconces
emotion: still, reverent menace — a weapon at rest but watching
